slides.title: Cardio or lifting *for fat loss*?
slides.sub: Everyone argues about it. The real answer is simpler than the internet makes it.

slides.title: Diet drives fat loss
slides.body: Neither burns much next to your fork. *The deficit* is where fat loss actually happens.

slides.title: Lifting keeps muscle
slides.body: In a deficit, *strength training* keeps the weight you lose fat, not muscle.

slides.title: Cardio adds a deficit
slides.body: Cardio *burns extra calories* and helps your heart. A useful tool, not the main one.

slides.title: Don't forget walking
slides.body: Daily steps quietly burn *more than your workout* with zero recovery cost.

slides.title: Lift, then walk
slides.body: *Lift 2–4x a week*, walk daily, add cardio you enjoy. That's the whole formula.

slides.title: Train smart. *Lose fat.*
slides.body: Vibe Health rolls your steps, food & sleep into one score — so your training actually adds up to results.

caption:
Cardio vs lifting is the wrong fight. Your fork settles it. 🏋️

Diet drives the deficit, lifting protects muscle, cardio and steps help. Do all four, stop overthinking. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health rolls your steps, food & sleep into one score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Cardio vs Lifting for Fat Loss: What Actually Works
